index.card_pokedex_desc: Search and browse every Pokémon — base stats, types, abilities, and move learnsets in one place.
index.card_party_checker_desc: Instantly check your team's type matchups and weaknesses. Essential for battle prep.
index.card_moves_desc: Search and browse every move. Power, accuracy, effects, and which Pokémon learn it — all at a glance.
index.card_type_chart_desc: Full 18-type matchup matrix with offensive coverage and weakness lists. See at a glance which moves hit super effectively.
index.cheer_text: Site mascot Pcham is cheering on your battle prep!
index.cheer_desc:
PchamDB is an unofficial fan database for Pokémon Champions —
built to help with battle prep and team building.
index.card_battle_desc: Damage calculation, OHKO checks, Focus Sash detection, and item modifiers in one place.
index.card_howto_desc: A complete guide to using each tool and getting the most out of the site. New here? Start with this.
index.guide_about_p: PchamDB is an unofficial fan database for Nintendo's competitive game Pokémon Champions. It brings together everything you need to prepare for battle—base stats, types, abilities, moves, type matchups, and damage calculation—all in one place, so you can build, look up, and confirm your team on a single site without jumping between page after page of strategy guides. Every feature is free to use right in your browser, with no sign-up or installation required. The site is also available in 9 languages, so Pokémon Champions fans all over the world—starting with Japanese and English speakers—can make the most of it.
index.guide_tool_db: Pokémon DB is a Pokédex-style tool for searching and browsing every Pokémon's base stats, types, abilities, and learnable moves in one list. Type a name or ability into the search box to filter, and click a stat column to sort by value. Click a name to open its details and move list—handy for finding the centerpiece of your team. Open · Detailed guide
index.guide_tool_checker: Team Builder lets you register your six Pokémon and automatically visualizes your whole team's type matchups, weaknesses, and offensive coverage. Gaps in your typing become obvious at a glance, and once you set natures and EVs it can calculate actual stats too. Save multiple teams as tabs to compare them side by side—ideal for finding the right partner while you fine-tune a build. Open · Detailed guide
index.guide_tool_waza: Move List lets you search every move by power, accuracy, PP, secondary effect, and priority, along with which Pokémon can learn it. You can sort by column header and filter by conditions such as priority moves or contact moves, making it easy to pick candidates for an open moveslot or refine a set. Open
index.guide_tool_type: Type Chart is a matrix showing the offensive and defensive matchups across all 18 types. You can view it from both the attacking and defending side, and sorting by fewest weaknesses instantly reveals which types are sturdiest on defense. It's also handy for seeing which moves hit super effectively and for working out dual-type matchups. Open
index.guide_tool_sim: Battle Simulator calculates damage based on actual stats, held items, and nature modifiers. It handles guaranteed KO ranges (how many hits it takes to faint a target), Focus Sash activation, and held-item multipliers. You can confirm bulk-investment thresholds and the outcome of a trade in hard numbers before you ever battle. Open
index.guide_for_p: It's ideal for competitive players who are serious about building teams for ranked matches and tournaments. It helps when you want to patch up matchup gaps, lock in a move set, or verify bulk thresholds with real numbers. It also works as an entry point for newcomers to learn how to read type matchups and base stats.
index.guide_data_p: The data shown here has been compiled independently and is revised as the game is updated and new information comes to light. We strive for accuracy, but updates may lag behind or errors may remain, so please double-check the in-game display when making final decisions. This is a personally run unofficial fan site with no affiliation whatsoever with Nintendo, The Pokémon Company, or any related companies. If you spot a mistake or have a request, please reach out via Contact.
index.card_real_battle_desc: A full battle experience with authentic visuals and pacing. Send 6 Pokémon from the Team Builder and battle turn by turn!
index.card_items_desc: Browse all items by effect, multiplier, and how to obtain them. Mega Stones, Berries, and Power Items organized by category.
index.card_news_desc: Latest updates. Announcements for new Pokémon, moves, items, and rule changes.
index.section_info_sub: Look up Pokémon, moves, Abilities, and types from each list.
index.card_pokemon_list_desc: Check each Pokémon's base stats, weaknesses, Abilities, and learnable moves on their individual pages.
index.card_ability_list_desc: Browse all Abilities and the Pokémon that have them.
index.section_national_sub: Not just Pokémon Champions — a complete edition covering every Pokémon, move, Ability, and item from all past generations.
index.card_national_pokedex_desc: Search and browse every Pokémon from all generations (National) by base stats, type, Ability, and move.
index.card_national_moves_desc: Every move from all generations. Narrow down in detail with effect tags like slicing moves, priority moves, and more.
index.card_national_items_desc: Browse effects and how to obtain every item — including past generations — organized by category.
index.card_national_abilities_desc: Effects of all 367 Abilities from every generation, with the Pokémon that have each one, all in one list.
index.card_national_artlist_desc: All 1,330 species with our original art and official sprites side by side. Search by name, filter by type.
index.card_online_desc: Battle other players online in real time! Build your team of 6 and find an opponent in the lobby — or use a passcode with a friend!

howto.page_title: How to Use - PchamDB (Unofficial Pokémon Champions Database)
howto.sub: How to use each tool and make the most of the site
howto.faq1_a: Yes. All features are free to use with no sign-up required.
howto.faq2_q: Is this the official Pokémon Champions site?
howto.faq2_a: No. It's a personally run, unofficial fan site with no affiliation to Nintendo, The Pokémon Company, or any related companies.
howto.faq3_q: Can I use it on a smartphone?
howto.faq3_a: Yes. It runs in any PC or smartphone browser—no app installation needed.
howto.faq4_a: Japanese, English, Spanish, French, German, Italian, Korean, and Chinese (Simplified / Traditional)—9 languages in total.
howto.faq5_q: How often is the data updated?
howto.faq5_a: The data is updated as the game receives updates and as new information comes to light.
howto.meta_desc: PchamDB user guide. Learn how to use the Pokédex, Team Builder, move list, type chart, and battle simulator, and how to get the most out of the site.

db_guide.page_title: How to Use the Pokédex - PchamDB (Unofficial Pokémon Champions Database)
db_guide.sub: Build a team and cover its weaknesses with the Battle Team (BT) feature
db_guide.intro: The Pokédex lets you view everything about every Pokémon — data, stats, weaknesses, and the moves they learn — all in a single list. I couldn't find a site like this anywhere, so I built it myself.
db_guide.feat1_p: See the total number of weaknesses, plus which types you resist or are immune to, at a glance. From this it derives a "bulk score" and "weakness score" — helping you judge whether a Pokémon is at a disadvantage with many weaknesses despite high stats, or is bulky and hard to take down.
db_guide.feat2_p: Because move data is included too, you can search Pokémon by combined conditions. For example, narrow down to Pokémon that learn Taunt and are faster than yours. You can also easily find Pokémon with healing moves, Whirlwind, and more.
db_guide.feat3_p: Pick candidate Pokémon for a Battle Team and it will automatically suggest well-matched partners that cover each other's weaknesses. The steps below explain how.
db_guide.feat_outro: Together, these features make it highly effective for assembling a team — an ideal data list for building your Battle Team.
db_guide.steps_h: How to use it (Battle Team feature)
db_guide.cap_overview: DB view: build teams with BT and analyze their combined weaknesses
db_guide.step1_p: Next to each Pokémon's name are the BT1, BT2, BT3 columns. Pick a number from 1 to 7 in a BT column to add that Pokémon as a candidate for that team. (The numbers 1–7 are candidate slots for team building.)
db_guide.cap_bt: Pick 1–7 in a BT column to register to a team
db_guide.step2_p: Open the coverage-search window from the Coverage ①/②/③ buttons. It suggests Pokémon whose typing covers the weaknesses of the Pokémon you checked into a BT. Check the BT1–3 numbers on the candidates to build your team.
db_guide.cap_compat_btn: Open coverage search from the Coverage ①–③ buttons
db_guide.cap_compat_win: Suggests well-matched Pokémon that cover your weaknesses
db_guide.step3_h: Update to add to your team
db_guide.step3_p: In the candidate list at the bottom, check the BT column for the Pokémon you want to add. Press the Update button at the top: the selected Pokémon are added to the team list above and fresh coverage candidates are recalculated.
db_guide.cap_update: "Update" adds them to your team and recalculates candidates
db_guide.step4_p: To clear all checks in a BT column, click the BT1 / BT2 / BT3 reset buttons. Pressing All resets everything except the BT entries (filters, etc.); your BT registrations are kept.
db_guide.cap_reset: All resets everything except BT; BT1–3 clear each team
db_guide.meta_desc: Guide to using the PchamDB Pokédex. Step-by-step with screenshots: sort your roster into Battle Teams (BT1/BT2/BT3) and cover weaknesses with the complement search.

builder_guide.page_title: How to Use the Team Builder - PchamDB (Unofficial Pokémon Champions Database)
builder_guide.h1: How to Use the Team Builder
builder_guide.sub: Pick Pokémon, moves, and stats to build your team
builder_guide.intro: The Team Builder is an all-in-one tool for assembling a strong team. Pick a Pokémon, explore the moves it can learn, and set base stats and actual stats (nature / EVs) to check your Pokémon's strength and how your points are allocated. Keeping this page open during a battle also helps you track what moves the opponent's Pokémon might have.
builder_guide.step1_h: Pick a Pokémon for a slot
builder_guide.step1_p: Choose a Pokémon from ＋ Select in each slot. You can search by name or Pokédex number, and Mega Evolutions and regional forms are available too.
builder_guide.cap_select: Search by name or number to pick a Pokémon
builder_guide.step2_p: Click the move area under a Pokémon's name to open the move list (Team Builder version). Check the moves you want from the ones it can learn, then confirm.
builder_guide.cap_move_list: Check moves in the move list and confirm
builder_guide.step3_p: Click the stats area to open the settings popup. Pick a nature and assign EVs to each stat, and the actual stats are calculated on the spot. The MIN / MAX buttons let you max out quickly.
builder_guide.cap_stat_popup: Set nature and EVs to calculate actual stats
builder_guide.step4_p: Use the Base / 6V / Nature / EV252 / Max / Rank buttons to show or hide each set of numbers. You can also drag a slot to move it to another slot.
builder_guide.cap_toggle: Show only the numbers you want with the toggle buttons
builder_guide.meta_desc: Guide to using the PchamDB Team Builder. With screenshots: pick Pokémon in each slot, set moves and stats (nature/EVs), and check your team’s power and point allocation.

type_chart.title_h1: Type Chart & Weakness Reference - Pokémon Champions - PchamDB (Unofficial)
type_chart.nav_db_title: Open Pokémon DB in new tab
type_chart.nav_checker_title: Open Team Builder in new tab
type_chart.nav_sim_title: Open Battle Simulator in new tab
type_chart.nav_waza_title: Open Move List in new tab
type_chart.atk_h2_sub: Rows: attacking move type / Columns: defending Pokémon type (single type)
type_chart.atk_note:
Default is canonical order (same as official). Use the # column on the left to return to canonical order at any time. Click the ●/▲/× count headers to sort by most/fewest.
The right-side lists show which types you can hit for ● super-effective, ▲ resisted, or × no-effect at a glance.
type_chart.def_h2_sub: Rows: defending Pokémon type (single type) / Columns: attacking move type
type_chart.def_note: Default is canonical order (same as official). Use the # column on the left to return to canonical order at any time. Click the weakness count column to sort by fewest weaknesses (most defensive first). Steel, Normal, and Electric have only 1–3 weaknesses (sturdy), while Grass and Rock have 5 (the most). Single-type evaluation; dual types are not considered.
type_chart.idx_tip: Click to restore default order (official order)

waza.nav_checker_title: Open Team Builder in new tab
waza.search_mode_title: Search mode for multi-chip: OR = any match, AND = all match
waza.select_mode_title: Chip selection mode: Single = click selects only one, Multi = click adds
waza.hint_sort_filter: * Header to sort, below to filter
waza.col_learners_title: Number of Pokémon that learn (sort by header, hover for full list)
waza.col_tags_title: Filter tags this move belongs to
waza.ef_guide_detail: 🏷 Detailed Tags (target specific effects · green band below)
waza.ef_guide_note: Example: Old "⚡ Paralysis" = all paralysis effects ↔ New "⚡ 30% paralysis" = 30% probability only. Combine with AND for filters like "Priority × 30% paralysis".
waza.ef_new_tag_hint: Filter by detailed effect (159 tags) ← finer than the "Quick Filter" above. Examples: 30% paralysis, self ATK+1, weather (rain), screen (Reflect).
waza.chip_punch_tip: Power x1.2 with the Iron Fist Ability
waza.chip_ball_tip: Ball and bomb moves blocked by the Bulletproof Ability
waza.chip_pulse_tip: Power x1.5 with the Mega Launcher Ability
waza.chip_charge_tip: Charges on turn 1, attacks on turn 2
waza.chip_recharge_tip: The user can't move on the turn after using this move (e.g., Hyper Beam)
waza.chip_exc_charge_tip: Hide moves that charge on turn 1 and attack on turn 2
waza.chip_exc_recharge_tip: Hide moves that leave the user unable to move the following turn
waza.chip_exc_self_rank_down_tip: Hide moves that lower the user's own stat stages after use (Close Combat / Superpower / Shell Smash, etc.)
waza.chip_exc_recoil_tip: Hide moves that deal recoil damage to the user (Take Down, etc.)
waza.chip_exc_fail_damage_tip: Hide moves that deal heavy damage to the user if they miss (High Jump Kick, etc.)
waza.chip_exc_self_faint_tip: Hide moves that cause the user to faint (Explosion / Final Gambit, etc.)
waza.chip_order_priority_hi_tip: Moves with positive priority that strike before most others
waza.chip_order_priority_lo_tip: Moves with negative priority that always go last
waza.chip_order_charge_tip: Moves that charge on turn 1 and attack on turn 2
waza.chip_order_recharge_tip: Moves that leave the user unable to act the next turn (Hyper Beam, etc.)
waza.chip_crit_hi_tip: Moves with critical-hit stage +1 or higher (easier to land critical hits)
waza.chip_crit_sure_tip: Moves that always result in a critical hit
waza.chip_always_hit_tip: Moves that bypass accuracy and evasion checks (Shadow Punch / Aerial Ace, etc.)
waza.chip_multihit_tip: Hits multiple times in one turn (fixed 2 hits / random 2-5 hits)
waza.chip_rampage_tip: Attacks for 2-3 turns then confuses the user (Outrage / Thrash, etc.)
waza.chip_recoil_tip: User takes a portion of the damage dealt as recoil (1/2, 1/3, 1/4)
waza.chip_fail_damage_tip: User takes 1/2 max HP damage if the move misses or is blocked (High Jump Kick, etc.)
waza.chip_heal_only_tip: Directly restores HP of the user, ally, or target (excludes drain moves)
waza.chip_drain_tip: Absorbs a portion of damage dealt to restore the user's HP
waza.chip_heal_status_tip: Heals status conditions for self / ally / foe (Heal Bell / Rest / Sparkling Aria, etc.)
waza.chip_self_faint_tip: Moves that cause the user to faint (Explosion / Self-Destruct / Final Gambit, etc.)
waza.chip_bind_tip: Traps the target for 4-5 turns, dealing 1/8 max HP damage each turn
waza.chip_force_switch_tip: Forces the target to switch out
waza.chip_self_switch_tip: User switches out after using the move (U-turn, etc.)
waza.chip_trap_tip: Prevents the target from switching out or fleeing
waza.chip_move_lock_tip: Restricts the target's move usage (Disable / Encore / Taunt / Silence move seal)
waza.chip_weather_tip: Changes the weather (Sunny / Rain / Sandstorm / Snow)
waza.chip_terrain_tip: Changes the terrain (Electric / Grassy / Psychic / Misty)
waza.chip_room_tip: Trick Room / Wonder Room / Magic Room
waza.chip_tailwind_tip: Doubles Speed of all allies for 4 turns (Tailwind)
waza.chip_hazard_tip: Sets Spikes / Stealth Rock / Toxic Spikes on the foe's side
waza.chip_screen_tip: Light Screen / Reflect / Aurora Veil / Safeguard (lasts 5 turns)
waza.chip_wide_guard_tip: Protects allies for 1 turn (Wide Guard / Quick Guard, priority +3)
waza.chip_guard_pierce_tip: Ignores Protect / Detect and similar moves (Whirlwind / Roar / Shadow Force / Baby-Doll Eyes)
waza.chip_hazard_clear_tip: Removes entry hazards from the field (Defog / Rapid Spin / Tidy Up / Mortal Spin)
waza.chip_terrain_clear_tip: Destroys the active terrain (Steel Roller / Ice Spinner)
waza.chip_screen_break_tip: Destroys Light Screen / Reflect / Aurora Veil then attacks (Brick Break / Psychic Fangs / Raging Bull)
waza.chip_rank_reset_tip: Resets all stat stages (Haze / Clear Smog)
waza.chip_support_dbl_tip: Status moves useful in Doubles (Helping Hand / Follow Me, etc.). W = Doubles
waza.chip_rank_manip_tip: Copies or swaps stat stages (Psych Up / Power Swap, etc.)
waza.chip_sub_pierce_tip: Moves that ignore Substitute and hit through it (21 moves)
waza.col_gen_title: Generation when the move was introduced (~ = up to current Gen9 / removed moves show last gen)
waza.new_only_tip: Show only moves newly added in the National version (non M-A/M-B)

news.header_sub: Pokémon Champions Regulation & New Content Announcements
news.card1_title: Regulation M-B Begins — 38 Pokémon + 8 Moves + 27 Items Added!
news.label_normal_items: 11 Regular Items (15 items newly supported including 4 existing ones)
news.rule_mega_limit_body: Only 1 Mega Evolution is allowed per battle (having multiple Mega Stones on your team is OK)
news.rule_mega_method_body: Activate with the R button (or select from the UI) when choosing a move. Does not use up your turn.
news.rule_form_body: The Mega Evolved form is kept until the battle ends.
news.card2_title: Detailed Tag Overhaul & Sim Test Enhancements
news.card2_bullet1: Redesigned Move List detail tags to match the old filter categories (20+ categories)
news.card2_bullet2: Status condition tags restored; sort order unified by stat (Atk→Def→Sp.Atk→Sp.Def→Speed)
news.card2_bullet3: Sim test stage 143 added: 38 new Reg M-B Pokémon verified, all 185 party battle combos successful
news.card2_bullet4: New tags added: "Charge/multi-turn move", "Priority move", "Last move", "Recoil damage", "Multi-hit", and more
news.card2_bullet5: New cross-check tool added (auto-verifies consistency between old filters and detail tags)
news.fx_dogeza: Priority +1 · needs damage taken last turn · Grimmsnarl
news.fx_hikkuri: Reverse foe's stat changes · Malamar line
news.fx_haisui: Raise own 5 stats +1 · can't switch · Falinks line
news.card0_title: National Dex DB Released — All Generations: 1,302 Pokémon + 920 Moves + Better Multilingual Support!
news.card0_bullet1: National Pokédex DB released (all generations, 1,302 Pokémon). Browse base stats, type matchups, abilities and moves in one all-in-one table.
news.card0_bullet2: National move list (920 moves), ability list (367 abilities) and item DB released at the same time.
news.card0_bullet3: Major multilingual improvements: Pokémon names, move names and ability descriptions now display in 9 languages.
news.card0_bullet4: Battle simulator accuracy improved: effect data for 427 newly added moves refined, powering both descriptions and battle simulation.
news.card_rb_title: ⚔ Real Battle major update — all-new Champions-style UI!
news.card_rb_b1: Battle screen redesigned in Pokémon Champions style (opponent HP %, move capsules, effectiveness hints ★◎△✕, attack animations). Team-select screen now uses the 3-column layout too.
news.card_rb_b2: Switch character art between our original drawings and official-style sprites (PokéAPI) — regional forms and Megas supported.
news.card_rb_b3: Audited usage conditions for all 490 moves and fixed them to match the games (Belch, Sucker Punch, Focus Punch, Outrage, etc.). Fling and Last Resort implemented.

making.page_title: Behind the Scenes — How We Built It in a Month with Claude Code
making.hero_sub: Pi-chan tells the story of how PchamDB came to life 🐉
making.trigger_p1: When Pokémon Champions was announced, we thought: “We want a tool that makes battle prep easier.” Existing fan sites have tons of information, but we couldn’t find a tool that lets you see battle-relevant info—type matchups, team coverage, move effect filters—side by side on one screen.
making.trigger_strong_one_screen: side by side on one screen
making.trigger_p2: “If it doesn’t exist, let’s build it.” That’s how things got started in April 2026.
making.quote_intro: But we’re not hardcore engineers. We’d only dabbled in programming. Building everything alone felt really tough. That’s when we turned to Claude Code.
making.stack_claude_code_role: Main coding partner. Handles file editing and execution end-to-end via CLI
making.stack_html_role: Built with plain HTML + JS, no frameworks. Easy to host as static files
making.stack_github_role: Published free with a custom domain (pchamdb.com). CDN included
making.stack_pokeapi_role: Source for official translation data used for multilingual support
making.stack_rakuten_role: A recommendation widget to help cover running costs
making.stack_google_forms_role: Contact form, with reCAPTCHA for spam protection
making.week1_title: Starting by Building the Master DB
making.week1_desc: We cross-referenced each Pokémon's base stats, types, Abilities, and learnable moves against official sources and multiple strategy sites. This part took careful manual work. Since AI can introduce errors, a human always makes the final call on data accuracy.
making.week2_desc: Built a UI with Claude Code that lets you scroll through every Pokémon side by side. Filters, sorting, and color-coded type matchups — improved a little each day by using it hands-on.
making.week3_title: Check Type Coverage in an Instant
making.week3_desc: Enter your team of 6 and see type matchups, attack coverage, and weaknesses to fill at a glance. We packed in a lot of information so competitive players can use it for final team-building tweaks.
making.week4_desc: OGP, Twitter Cards, JSON-LD structured data, sitemap.xml, canonical URLs — with Claude Code's help, we laid the SEO groundwork. We also fine-tuned details like page navigation, a back-to-top button, and a mirrored horizontal scrollbar.
making.commitment_accuracy_desc: The master DB is always verified against official sources by a human. We never hand that job off to AI.
making.commitment_unofficial_desc: Every page clearly states '⚠️ This is an unofficial fan site.' Trademarks and copyrights belong to their respective owners.
making.commitment_lightweight_desc: No frameworks used. Pure HTML/CSS/JS for blazing-fast page loads.
making.commitment_mobile_desc: Horizontal scrolling lets you view all data on a smartphone too.
making.hardship_mirror_scroll: Horizontal scrollbar hidden below a giant table → fixed with a mirror scrollbar
making.hardship_fetch: fetch() not working with local files → implemented a fallback
making.hardship_jsonld: Validation hook treating JSON-LD as a JS error → extended the hook
making.hardship_ai_communication: 'How do we talk about building with AI?' — still figuring that one out
making.future_p1: The site is currently Japanese-only, but we're working on multilingual support (English, Chinese, Korean, and more). We're pulling official translation data from PokéAPI and preparing support for 9 languages.
making.future_multilang_strong: multilingual support (English, Chinese, Korean, and more)
making.future_p2: We're also planning a Battle Simulator and a community team-sharing feature down the road. The challenge of seeing how far one person + AI can go continues.
making.series_intro: We write about the making of this site and our thoughts on both note and Zenn. note is more story-focused, while Zenn is more technical. We plan to keep the series going with #2, #3, and beyond.
making.series_note_article1_title: #1 How I Launched a Pokémon Battle DB in One Month with Claude AI — Without Writing a Single Line of Code
making.series_note_article1_desc: From a chance encounter at a crane game, to discovering Claude after Sora shut down, to the origin story rooted in Satoshi Tajiri's strategy guide — a 6,000-word making-of
making.series_zenn_article1_title: #1 Shipping a Battle DB Site with Claude Code and Plain HTML/JS
making.series_zenn_article1_desc: No frameworks + Claude Code: 3 technical tricky spots (mirror scrollbar / JSON-LD hook / fetch fallback) + SEO implementation details
making.quote_closing: PchamDB was made with the hope of making battle prep for Pokémon Champions just a little easier. If you try it out and have any thoughts or requests, please let us know via the contact form.
making.trigger_p1_html: It all started when Pokémon Champions was announced and I thought, "I want a tool that makes preparing for battles easier." Existing strategy sites are packed with information, but I had a hard time finding a tool that lets you see everything side by side on one screen—the stuff that matters most for battling (type matchups, team coverage, move-effect filters).
making.quote_claude_html: But I'm not exactly a hardcore engineer. I've only dabbled a little in programming. Honestly, building it all alone is tough. So I turned to Claude Code.
making.week1_desc_html: I cross-checked each Pokémon's base stats, types, abilities, and learnable moves against official sources and several strategy sites. This part was painstaking manual work. Leaving it to the AI lets hallucinations creep in, so I adopted a policy where a human always has the final say on what's correct in the data.
making.hardship_fetch_html: The problem of fetch() not working with local files → implemented a fallback
making.future_multilang_html: Right now it's Japanese only, but I'm working on multilingual support (English, Chinese, Korean, etc.). I'm pulling official translation data from PokeAPI to prepare support for 9 languages.
making.future_battlesim_html: Down the line, I'm also envisioning a battle simulator and a user-submitted team-sharing feature. With just one person + AI, the challenge of seeing how far I can take this continues.
making.series_p_html: I write about my creative process and the things on my mind on both note and Zenn. note leans toward the story side, while Zenn leans toward the technical side—I write differently for each. I plan to keep this going with #2, #3, and beyond.
making.closing_quote_html: I built PchamDB hoping it would make preparing for Pokémon Champions battles even a little bit easier. If you have any thoughts or requests after trying it out, I'd love to hear them via the contact form.

privacy.meta_description: Privacy Policy for PchamDB. Information about cookies, access analytics, and affiliate programs.
privacy.intro: PchamDB ("this site") respects user privacy and is committed to protecting personal information. This policy describes how information is handled on this site.
privacy.section1_intro: This site may collect the following information.
privacy.log_purpose: This information is used solely for improving site operations and statistical analysis.
privacy.cookie_intro: This site uses cookies and LocalStorage to improve the user experience.
privacy.localstorage_desc: LocalStorage: Checklist state (C1/C2/C3, etc.) is saved only in your browser. It is never sent to the server.
privacy.cookie_desc: Cookies: May be used for access analytics and ad delivery (see below for details).
privacy.cookie_opt_out: You can disable cookies in your browser settings. Most features of this site will still work without cookies, but some features may be limited.
privacy.personal_info_desc: This site does not directly collect personally identifiable information such as names or email addresses, except when a user reaches out via the contact form.
privacy.analytics_intro: This site uses access analytics tools to understand how the site is used.
privacy.ga_desc: This site uses Google Analytics, provided by Google LLC. This tool uses cookies to collect traffic data. The traffic data is collected anonymously and does not identify individuals.
privacy.ga_optout_text: If you want to disable Google Analytics, you can do so by installing the
privacy.cloudflare_desc: This site may use Cloudflare Web Analytics, provided by Cloudflare, Inc. This service collects access statistics in a privacy-friendly way without using cookies.
privacy.amazon_affiliate_desc: This site participates in the Amazon Associates Program, an affiliate advertising program designed to provide a means for sites to earn referral fees by advertising and linking to Amazon.co.jp.
privacy.amazon_link_desc: Amazon.co.jp links on this site are provided through the Amazon Associates Program. If a product is purchased through a link, this site may receive a referral fee from Amazon.co.jp.
privacy.amazon_cookie_desc: Through the Amazon Associates Program, Amazon.co.jp uses cookies to collect access information when users visit Amazon product pages. For details on the information collected, please see the
privacy.rakuten_affiliate_desc: This site participates in the Rakuten Affiliate Program operated by Rakuten Group, Inc. We may receive a referral fee when products from Rakuten Ichiba are purchased through links on this site.
privacy.rakuten_cookie_desc: When you click a Rakuten affiliate link, Rakuten uses cookies to collect access information. For details on the information collected, please see the
privacy.third_party_intro: This site does not share collected information with third parties except in the following cases.
privacy.third_party_item1: When the user has given consent
privacy.third_party_item2: When disclosure is required by law
privacy.third_party_item3: When necessary to protect a person's life, body, or property, and it is difficult to obtain consent
privacy.third_party_item4: When shared with service providers (Google Analytics, Cloudflare, Amazon, etc.) to the extent necessary for providing the service
privacy.external_link_desc: This site may contain links to third-party websites. This site's privacy policy does not apply to information handled on linked websites. Please review the privacy policy of any linked website you visit.
privacy.disclaimer_desc: This site accepts no liability for any damages arising from your use of this site. For details, please see the
privacy.revision_desc: This site may update this Privacy Policy as needed. Any changes take effect as soon as the revised policy is posted on this site.
privacy.contact_desc: For inquiries about this policy, please use the contact form. We do not publish an email address. To prevent spam, all inquiries are accepted through a Google Form.
privacy.footer_unofficial: This is an unofficial fan site
privacy.footer_disclaimer: This site is not affiliated with Nintendo, The Pokémon Company, Game Freak, Creatures Inc., or any related companies.

terms.intro: These Terms of Use (the "Terms") set out the conditions for using the services provided by PchamDB (the "Site"). All users of the Site ("Users") are deemed to have agreed to these Terms.
terms.article1_body:
These Terms apply to all relationships between Users and the Site administrator regarding use of the Site.
    Any individual rules or notices posted on the Site form part of these Terms.

terms.article2_highlight_body:
This site is not affiliated with Nintendo Co., Ltd., The Pokémon Company, Game Freak, Inc., Creatures Inc.,

    For the latest and most accurate information, please always check official sources.

terms.article3_item2: Not engage in any activity that interferes with the operation of the Site

terms.article4_item1: Acts that violate laws, regulations, or public morality
terms.article4_item3: Acts that infringe on the intellectual property rights, portrait rights, privacy, reputation, or other rights or interests of the Site administrator, other Users, or third parties
terms.article4_item4: Acts that destroy or interfere with the servers or network functions of the Site
terms.article4_item5: Unauthorized access or attempts to gain unauthorized access
terms.article4_item6: Reproducing, reposting, or redistributing Site content without permission
terms.article4_item7: Altering information posted on the Site and redistributing it
terms.article4_item8: Any other acts that the Site administrator deems inappropriate
terms.article5_body:
The copyrights and other intellectual property rights to the text, images, logos, character illustrations (original characters such as "Pi-chan"),
    source code, database structure, and other content on this Site belong to

terms.article6_body_prefix:
This Site may include affiliate links such as the Amazon Associates Program.
    For details, please see our
terms.article7_intro:
The Site administrator may suspend or interrupt all or part of the Site without prior notice to Users
    if any of the following circumstances apply.
terms.article7_item1: When performing system maintenance or updates related to the Site
terms.article7_item2: When providing the service becomes difficult due to earthquakes, lightning, fire, power outages, or other natural disasters
terms.article7_item3: When computers or communication lines are stopped due to an accident
terms.article7_item4: In any other case where the Site administrator determines that providing the service is difficult for operational or technical reasons
terms.article7_disclaimer:
The Site administrator shall bear no responsibility for any disadvantage or damage suffered by Users or third parties
    due to the suspension or interruption of the service.
terms.article8_body_prefix:
We do not guarantee the accuracy, completeness, or usefulness of the information on this Site.
    For details, please see our

The Site administrator may change these Terms without prior notice to Users when deemed necessary.
    The revised Terms shall take effect from the time they are posted on the Site.

terms.article10_body:
These Terms shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of Japan.
    In the event of any dispute arising in connection with this Site, the court with jurisdiction over the location of the Site administrator shall have exclusive jurisdiction.
terms.footer_unofficial: ⚠️ This is an unofficial fan site
terms.footer_disclaimer_body:
This site is not affiliated with Nintendo, The Pokémon Company, Game Freak, Creatures Inc.,
    or any related companies.

contact.intro_request: For feedback, questions, or reports of incorrect information, please use the form below.
contact.form_cta_text: We accept inquiries via Google Form
contact.form_cta_login_note: A Google account login may be required to prevent spam
contact.form_note_new_tab: * The Google Form will open in a new tab.
contact.form_note_login: * You may need to log in with a Google account.
contact.welcome_section_title: We welcome these kinds of messages
contact.topic_bug_title: Reporting incorrect information or data errors
contact.topic_bug_desc: If you find an error in move, Ability, base stats, type, or other data
contact.topic_feature_desc: Ideas for features you'd like to see
contact.topic_report_desc: Issues such as broken layouts or malfunctions
contact.topic_media_desc: Requests for media coverage or features
contact.topic_feedback_desc: We love hearing how your experience was!
contact.request_bug_detail: : Please include the item in question (e.g., "PP for move [X]"), what is currently shown, and the correct information.
contact.request_bugreport_detail: : Please let us know the page where the issue occurred, the steps to reproduce it, and your browser and OS.
contact.request_feature_detail: : Please describe the situation where you'd need it and how you'd like to use it.
contact.reply_note_strong: This site is run by one person, so replies may take some time.
contact.reply_note_detail:
Please note that we may not be able to reply to every message.
    In particular, we may skip replies to "thank you" messages and general feedback.
contact.security_intro: This site uses Google Forms for the contact form as a spam prevention measure.
contact.security_item_limit: Flood prevention (limit of one submission per user)
contact.caution_no_strategy: We are unable to assist with gameplay strategy or battle advice.
contact.caution_official_redirect: For inquiries about the official Pokémon series, please visit
contact.caution_no_spam: We do not respond to spam, solicitations, or unrelated inquiries
contact.caution_no_email: This site does not publish an email address. Please use the contact form above
contact.footer_links_before: Before contacting us, please review our
contact.footer_unofficial: ⚠️ This is an unofficial fan site
contact.footer_disclaimer:
This site is not affiliated with Nintendo, The Pokémon Company, GAME FREAK,

ability.search_ph: 🔍 Filter by ability name or effect
ability.col_pokemon_title: No. of Pokémon with this Ability (click for list)

online_battle.setup_heading: Pick your battle team (3–6) from the Box on the right, then battle from the 🌐 Lobby below! (Moves are set automatically — edit details in the middle panel)
online_battle.pick_title: Team preview! Pick your 3 in order
online_battle.box_preview_note: Box Pokémon (not on your team). Any build you set here is remembered
online_battle.pick_timeout_auto: Time's up! The rest were picked automatically
online_battle.mega_view_note: Mega Evolved form. It transforms with a Mega Stone during battle (cannot be added to your team)

battle_lab.setup_heading: 🧪 Battle Lab: Build both sides freely! Any move, ability, or item goes (⚙ Edit Details to change)
battle_lab.confirm_full_reset: This will reset everything, including your teams. Are you sure?

suggest.intro: Pick the Pokémon on your team, and this page sums up your team's shared weaknesses and suggests partners that cover them, ranked by score. Built for quickly answering “who else should I add?” on your phone.
suggest.selected_empty: Search for a Pokémon above and select it to see your team's weaknesses and suggested partners here.
suggest.weakness_hint: Red outline = a type at least 2 Pokémon are weak to (needs coverage). The number shows how much the weaknesses overlap (a 4× weakness counts as 2).
suggest.weakness_need_select: Select Pokémon to see the types your team is commonly weak to here.
suggest.ranking_hint: Score = tally of dangerous types: resisted (+2) / immune (+3) / neutral (0) / weak (−2). Ties are broken by higher total base stats. Tap a row to add it to your selection.
suggest.ranking_empty: Select Pokémon to see suggested partners that cover your weaknesses here.
suggest.ranking_no_danger: No suggestions — your team has no weaknesses to cover.
suggest.ranking_no_good: No candidates found that cover your weaknesses well. Try a different selection.
